spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates excellent spec alignment by using light-dark() with 'none' to conditionally show/hide decorative backgrounds (watermarks, starfields) based on the active color-scheme.

interactive.rationale: Includes interactive buttons to toggle between System, Light, and Dark modes, propagating the color-scheme down to the preview areas.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Parent blurb matches the conditional decorations concept.

edge_cases.rationale: Effectively tests the 'none' keyword in light-dark() for background-image, which is a crucial edge case for conditional UI assets.

references.rationale: Includes the required ChromeStatus reference link.

design_system.rationale: Uses design system variables for typography and borders.

openQuestions.title: Remove redundant JS color-scheme propagation
openQuestions.detail: In the `<script>` block (lines 308-310), the code loops through all descendants of `#demo-root` and sets their `colorScheme` inline style. Since `color-scheme` naturally inherits in CSS, setting it on `#demo-root` is sufficient. This redundant DOM looping should be removed.

openQuestions.title: Remove unused CSS classes
openQuestions.detail: The CSS classes `.force-light` and `.force-dark` (lines 160-161) are defined in the style block but never used in the HTML. They should be removed to keep the code clean.

summary: A very useful demo illustrating the 'none' keyword in light-dark() for conditionally showing decorative assets like watermarks and starfields. The interactivity works well. There are minor cleanups needed for redundant JS and unused CSS classes.
